iteration_1.description: Initial rewrite: short SKILL.md with python-pptx code examples, removed html2pptx/ooxml content

iteration_2.description: Put code in description field (like successful docx skill), added bundled extract_pptx.py script, emphasized DO NOT call tool

iteration_4.description: Reverted to document-pptx name, refined description emphasis on bundled script

iteration_5.description: Final version: added complete extraction code example with financial parsing regex patterns in SKILL.md body, API 503 errors limited results
iteration_5.note: Most runs hit Mistral 503 errors; limited valid results

changes_made: Replaced 484-line verbose SKILL.md with ~100-line SLM-optimized version
Removed all html2pptx, OOXML editing, template workflow content
Put python-pptx code directly in the description field (always visible in system prompt)
Added 'Do NOT call document-pptx as a tool' warning
Bundled scripts/extract_pptx.py for JSON extraction with financial data parsing
Removed 40+ XSD schema files, html2pptx.js, inventory.py, rearrange.py, replace.py, thumbnail.py, ooxml.md, html2pptx.md
Added complete extraction code example with regex patterns for parsing $4.2M and 23% formats
key_findings: devstral-small frequently garbles tool names with }() suffix, getting stuck in loops
Model never loads skills via <load-skill> tag — works purely from description
pptx-create is reliable (100% when API available) due to simpler task structure
pptx-extract has high variance (~33% success) due to model's inconsistent tool usage
When extract succeeds, model writes own script rather than using bundled script
Financial data parsing is the main failure mode — model writes buggy regex
The 'document-pptx' name gives better create reliability than 'pptx' name
